place: Copake Falls CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Copake Falls sits at the base of the Taconic Mountains where Bash Bish Brook drops from a scenic waterfall.
Settled in the 1830s, the hamlet grew around iron mining and smelting; the iron works closed over a century ago.
The historic Roeliff Jansen Historical Society Museum chronicles local history and hosts a 2026 Summer Exhibition titled “Patriots, Plowshares & Post Roads.”
Copake Falls hosts an annual “Copake Falls Day” each third Saturday of August, inviting residents and visitors to crafts, food, and fun.
The Copake Memorial Clock, added to the National Register of Historic Places in 2012, stands as a landmark in the hamlet.
The hamlet’s location in a flat valley along Bashbish Brook places it at the western foot of the Taconic Mountains, a range that borders New York and Massachusetts.
